006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with

015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.marshall.cp;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.CollectionUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.FileUtils.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.IoUtils.*;
022import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.*;
023import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.StringUtils.*;
024import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.StringUtils.isEmpty;
025
026import java.io.*;
027import java.util.*;
028import java.util.ResourceBundle.*;
029import java.util.concurrent.*;
030import java.util.regex.*;
031
032import org.apache.juneau.commons.collections.*;
033import org.apache.juneau.commons.io.*;
034
035/**
036 * Basic implementation of a {@link FileFinder}.
037 *
038 * <p>
039 * Specialized behavior can be implemented by overridding the {@link #find(String, Locale)} method.
040 *
041 * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
042 * <p class='bjava'>
043 *    <jk>public class</jk> MyFileFinder <jk>extends</jk> BasicFileFinder {
044 *       <ja>@Override</ja>
045 *       <jk>protected</jk> Optional&lt;InputStream&gt; find(String <jv>name</jv>, Locale <jv>locale</jv>) <jk>throws</jk> IOException {
046 *          <jc>// Do special handling or just call super.find().</jc>
047 *          <jk>return super</jk>.find(<jv>name</jv>, <jv>locale</jv>);
048 *       }
049 *    }
050 * </p>
051 *
052 */
053@SuppressWarnings({
054   "resource", // Resource management handled externally
055   "java:S115", // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention (e.g., PROP_cachingLimit)
056})
057public class BasicFileFinder implements FileFinder {
058
059   // Property name constants
060   private static final String PROP_cachingLimit = "cachingLimit";
061   private static final String PROP_class = "class";
062   private static final String PROP_exclude = "exclude";
063   private static final String PROP_hashCode = "hashCode";
064   private static final String PROP_include = "include";
065   private static final String PROP_roots = "roots";
066
067   private static final ResourceBundle.Control RB_CONTROL = ResourceBundle.Control.getControl(Control.FORMAT_DEFAULT);
068
069   private final Map<String,LocalFile> files = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();
070   private final Map<Locale,Map<String,LocalFile>> localizedFiles = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();
071
072   private final LocalDir[] roots;
073   private final long cachingLimit;
074   private final Pattern[] include;
075   private final Pattern[] exclude;
076   private final String[] includePatterns;
077   private final String[] excludePatterns;
078   private final int hashCode;
079
080   /**
081    * Builder-based constructor.
082    *
083    * @param builder The builder object.
084    */
085   public BasicFileFinder(FileFinder.Builder builder) {
086      this.roots = builder.roots.toArray(new LocalDir[builder.roots.size()]);
087      this.cachingLimit = builder.cachingLimit;
088      this.include = builder.include;
089      this.exclude = builder.exclude;
090      this.includePatterns = l(include).stream().map(Pattern::pattern).toArray(String[]::new);
091      this.excludePatterns = l(exclude).stream().map(Pattern::pattern).toArray(String[]::new);
092      this.hashCode = h(getClass(), roots, cachingLimit, includePatterns, excludePatterns);
093   }
094
095   /**
096    * Default constructor.
097    *
098    * <p>
099    * Can be used when providing a subclass that overrides the {@link #find(String, Locale)} method.
100    */
101   protected BasicFileFinder() {
102      this.roots = new LocalDir[0];
103      this.cachingLimit = -1;
104      this.include = new Pattern[0];
105      this.exclude = new Pattern[0];
106      this.includePatterns = new String[0];
107      this.excludePatterns = new String[0];
108      this.hashCode = h(getClass(), roots, cachingLimit, includePatterns, excludePatterns);
109   }
110
111   @Override /* Overridden from Object */
112   public boolean equals(Object o) {
113      return o instanceof BasicFileFinder o2 && eq(this, o2, (x, y) -> eq(x.hashCode, y.hashCode) && eq(x.getClass(), y.getClass()) && eq(x.roots, y.roots) && eq(x.cachingLimit, y.cachingLimit)
114         && eq(x.includePatterns, y.includePatterns) && eq(x.excludePatterns, y.excludePatterns));
115   }
116
117   @Override /* Overridden from FileFinder */
118   public final Optional<InputStream> getStream(String name, Locale locale) throws IOException {
119      return find(name, locale);
120   }
121
122   @Override /* Overridden from FileFinder */
123   public Optional<String> getString(String name, Locale locale) throws IOException {
124      return o(read(find(name, locale).orElse(null)));
125   }
126
127   @Override
128   public int hashCode() {
129      return hashCode;
130   }
131
132   protected FluentMap<String,Object> properties() {
133      // @formatter:off
134      return filteredBeanPropertyMap()
135         .a(PROP_cachingLimit, cachingLimit)
136         .a(PROP_class, cns(getClass()))
137         .a(PROP_exclude, excludePatterns)
138         .a(PROP_include, includePatterns)
139         .a(PROP_roots, roots)
140         .a(PROP_hashCode, hashCode);
141      // @formatter:on
142   }
143
144   @Override /* Overridden from Object */
145   public String toString() {
146      return r(properties());
147   }
148
149   /**
150    * The main implementation method for finding files.
151    *
152    * <p>
153    * Subclasses can override this method to provide their own handling.
154    *
155    * @param name The resource name.
156    *    See {@link Class#getResource(String)} for format.
157    * @param locale
158    *    The locale of the resource to retrieve.
159    *    <br>If <jk>null</jk>, won't look for localized file names.
160    * @return The resolved resource contents, or <jk>null</jk> if the resource was not found.
161    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
162    */
163   @SuppressWarnings({
164      "null", // Null handling verified by context or framework
165      "java:S3776", // Cognitive complexity acceptable for this specific logic
166   })
167   protected Optional<InputStream> find(String name, Locale locale) throws IOException {
168      name = trimSlashesAndSpaces(name);
169
170      if (isInvalidPath(name))
171         return oe();
172
173      if (nn(locale))
174         localizedFiles.putIfAbsent(locale, new ConcurrentHashMap<>());
175
176      Map<String,LocalFile> fileCache = locale == null ? files : localizedFiles.get(locale);
177
178      LocalFile lf = fileCache.get(name);
179
180      if (lf == null) {
181         List<String> candidateFileNames = getCandidateFileNames(name, locale);
182         for (LocalDir root : roots) {
183            for (var cfn : candidateFileNames) {
184               lf = root.resolve(cfn);
185               if (nn(lf))
186                  break;
187            }
188            if (nn(lf))
189               break;
190         }
191
192         if (nn(lf) && isIgnoredFile(lf.getName()))
193            lf = null;
194
195         if (nn(lf)) {
196            fileCache.put(name, lf);
197
198            if (cachingLimit >= 0) {
199               long size = lf.size();
200               if (size > 0 && size <= cachingLimit)
201                  lf.cache();
202            }
203         }
204      }
205
206      return o(lf == null ? null : lf.read());
207   }
208
209   /**
210    * Returns the candidate file names for the specified file name in the specified locale.
211    *
212    * <p>
213    * For example, if looking for the <js>"MyResource.txt"</js> file in the Japanese locale, the iterator will return
214    * names in the following order:
215    * <ol>
216    *    <li><js>"MyResource_ja_JP.txt"</js>
217    *    <li><js>"MyResource_ja.txt"</js>
218    *    <li><js>"MyResource.txt"</js>
219    * </ol>
220    *
221    * <p>
222    * If the locale is <jk>null</jk>, then it will only return <js>"MyResource.txt"</js>.
223    *
224    * @param fileName The name of the file to get candidate file names on.
225    * @param locale
226    *    The locale.
227    *    <br>If <jk>null</jk>, won't look for localized file names.
228    * @return An iterator of file names to look at.
229    */
230   protected List<String> getCandidateFileNames(String fileName, Locale locale) {
231
232      if (locale == null)
233         return Collections.singletonList(fileName);
234
235      var list = new ArrayList<String>();
236      var baseName = getBaseName(fileName);
237      var ext = getFileExtension(fileName);
238
239      getCandidateLocales(locale).forEach(x -> {
240         var ls = x.toString();
241         if (ls.isEmpty())
242            list.add(fileName);
243         else {
244            list.add(baseName + "_" + ls + (ext.isEmpty() ? "" : ('.' + ext)));
245            list.add(ls.replace('_', '/') + '/' + fileName);
246         }
247      });
248
249      return list;
250   }
251
252   /**
253    * Returns the candidate locales for the specified locale.
254    *
255    * <p>
256    * For example, if <c>locale</c> is <js>"ja_JP"</js>, then this method will return:
257    * <ol>
258    *    <li><js>"ja_JP"</js>
259    *    <li><js>"ja"</js>
260    *    <li><js>""</js>
261    * </ol>
262    *
263    * @param locale The locale to get the list of candidate locales for.
264    * @return The list of candidate locales.
265    */
266   protected List<Locale> getCandidateLocales(Locale locale) {
267      return RB_CONTROL.getCandidateLocales("", locale);
268   }
269
270   /**
271    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the file should be ignored based on file name.
272    *
273    * @param name The name to check.
274    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the file should be ignored.
275    */
276   protected boolean isIgnoredFile(String name) {
277      for (var p : exclude)
278         if (p.matcher(name).matches())
279            return true;
280      for (var p : include)
281         if (p.matcher(name).matches())
282            return false;
283      return true;
284   }
285
286   /**
287    * Checks for path malformations such as use of <js>".."</js> which can be used to open up security holes.
288    *
289    * <p>
290    * Default implementation returns <jk>true</jk> if the path is any of the following:
291    * <ul>
292    *    <li>Is blank or <jk>null</jk>.
293    *    <li>Contains <js>".."</js> (to prevent traversing out of working directory).
294    *    <li>Contains <js>"%"</js> (to prevent URI trickery).
295    * </ul>
296    *
297    * @param path The path to check.
298    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the path is invalid.
299    */
300   protected boolean isInvalidPath(String path) {
301      return isEmpty(path) || path.contains("..") || path.contains("%");
302   }
303}
